heading: Inland Spousal and Common-Law Partner Sponsorship
description: Inland sponsorship enables Canadians and permanent residents to sponsor their spouse or common-law partner for permanent resident status when the couple is already living together in Canada. This comprehensive guide outlines the key components of Inland sponsorship, helping you determine if this spousal sponsorship category aligns with your needs.
kp1: Eligibility: Canadians and permanent residents can sponsor their loved ones, provided they legally cohabit in Canada.
kp2: Temporary Status Requirement: The foreign spouse or common-law partner does not need to hold valid temporary status in Canada (e.g., as a worker, student, or visitor).
kp3: Continued Living and Working: During the Inland sponsorship application process, the sponsored person can continue living, working, or studying in Canada.
kp4: PR Visa Approval: Both the Canadian citizen or permanent resident and the foreign national must be approved by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) for the sponsored person to receive a Canadian PR visa through Inland sponsorship.
b1: Open Work Permit (OWP): Applicants may qualify for an open work permit, allowing full-time employment while their permanent residence application is processed.
b2: Economic and Emotional Relief: An OWP eases financial and emotional burdens during the potentially lengthy application process.
b3: Staying in Canada: Inland sponsorship applicants are generally expected to remain in Canada throughout processing.
eligibleCriteriaHeading: Eligibility Criteria for Inland Spousal Sponsorship
e1: Your spouse or partner must live with you in Canada.
e2: Your spouse or partner must have temporary resident status in Canada.
e3: Both the sponsor and sponsored person must meet the basic requirements for immigration to Canada:
e3Sub1: The sponsor must be a Canadian citizen or permanent resident.
e3Sub2: Both parties must be at least 18 years old.
eligRelHeading: The relationship must fall into one of the following categories:
er1: Spouse: Legally married with a valid marriage certificate recognized under Canadian law.
er2: Common Law: Cohabiting in a marriage-like relationship for at least 12 consecutive months.
er3: The sponsored person cannot have been sponsored by their spouse in Canada within the past five years.
er4: Neither spouse must be in prison, charged with a serious offense, or bankrupt.
er5: Please note that conjugal partnerships (where religious or marital status barriers prevent living together or marriage) are not eligible for Inland sponsorship.
HowInlaSpoSponWorkHeading: How does Inland Spousal Sponsorship work?
HowInlaSpoSponWorkPara: Inland sponsorship is a specialized application process designed to sponsor a loved one under Canada’s Spouse or Common-Law Partner in Canada Class immigration program.
ac1: The Canadian citizen or permanent resident applies to become a sponsor.
ac2: The foreign spouse or partner applies for permanent residence.
HowAppInlSpoSponHeading: How to apply for Inland Spousal Sponsorship?
ha1: Verify eligibility for both sponsor and sponsored person.
ha4: Complete and submit the necessary forms to the appropriate visa office.
RemainingCanadaPara: Inland sponsorship applicants are generally expected to stay in Canada during processing. If the spouse or common-law partner needs to travel outside the country, Outland sponsorship may be a better option.
HowLongProcInlAppHeading: How long does it take to process Inland applications?
HowLongProcInlAppPara: Current approval times for Inland spousal sponsorship applications are approximately 12-14 months. Quebec residents or those planning to settle in Quebec may experience up to 24 months.
InlSpouSponHeading: Inland Spousal Sponsorship - Spousal Open Work Permit
InlSpouSponPara: The Spousal Open Work Permit Pilot Program (OWPP) allows spouses or common-law partners to work in Canada while awaiting spousal sponsorship application outcomes. Applicants can apply for an open work permit any time after applying for permanent residence in the Spouse or Common-Law Partner in Canada (SCLPC) class. The open work permit is not job-specific and allows work for any Canadian employer. These permits are typically valid for two years or until the passport expiry date.
EligOpenWorkHeading: Eligibility for Open Work Permit under the Pilot Program
eow1: Residency in Canada: You must currently reside in Canada.
eow2: Sponsorship Category: You need to be sponsored under the Spouse or Common-Law Partner in Canada (SCLPC) Class.
eow3: Valid Temporary Resident Status: Hold a valid temporary resident status, such as a visitor, student, or worker.
eow4: Same Address as Sponsor: You must live at the same address as your sponsor.
AppSpouHeading: Applying for a Spousal or Partner Work Permit
AppSpouPara: New spousal or partner work permit applications can be submitted along with your sponsorship application. These applications may also be submitted concurrently with an application for permanent residence. Ensure you include the appropriate fees and send the application by mail or courier.
ConjPartReqDocSponHeading: Common Law: Required Documents for Sponsorship
ConjPartReqDocSponPara: When applying, submit at least two of the following documents to validate your common-law relationship:
cprd5: Police certificates from countries where your spouse lived for six months or longer after turning 18.
cprd7: Proof of payment for government fees
cprd9: Relationship information and sponsorship evaluation questionnaire
cprd11: Birth certificates or adoption records for shared children.
cprd12: Proof of marriage registration with a government authority

q1: Can a Spouse Open Work Permit Be Extended?
qa1: A Spousal Open Work Permit (SOWP) is linked to the permit of the spouse (the ‘lead applicant’). It remains valid only as long as the lead applicant’s permit. If your SOWP expires, you’ll need to reapply based on your spouse’s permit.
q2: What is the main advantage of applying for Inland sponsorship?
qa2: The main benefit of opting for Inland sponsorship is that it allows your spouse or partner to apply for an Open Work Permit. Open work permits are not restricted to a particular job or location in Canada. Unlike other work permits, they do not necessitate a Labour Market Impact Assessment (LMIA).
q3: Will I be eligible for both Inland and Outland sponsorship if I live in Canada with my partner?
qa3: Yes. a couple may qualify for both Inland and Outland sponsorship if they are living together in Canada. If the couple is physically present in Canada when the application is started, but one spouse or partner intends to travel abroad during the application processing period, an Outland sponsorship application might be more appropriate. It's important to note that Outland sponsorship does not grant your spouse or partner the opportunity to obtain an open work permit for employment in Canada. Should you find yourself eligible for both sponsorship application categories, it's crucial to thoroughly evaluate and understand the various advantages and disadvantages associated with each option. The processing duration for open work permits is typically around 4 to 5 months.
q4: How long are you responsible for sponsoring a spouse in Canada?
qa4: The duration of the undertaking for sponsorship varies depending on the province, with a standard period of 3 years for sponsoring a spouse in all provinces except Quebec.
